texts: e mpfr::mpreal to a variable of type double. However, this operation is not allowed by the compiler due to the inability to convert 'const mpfr::mpreal' to 'double' in the assignment. To resolve this issue, you could use a static_cast to explicitly convert the value to a double: x = (typeid(T) == typeid(double) ? static_cast<double>(value) : value.toDouble()); This ensures that the value is correctly converted to a double when necessary. Here's a quick test program: #include <iostream> #include <typeinfo> #include "mpreal.h" template<class T> class Bla { private: double a; public: Bla(const T &x) { a = (std::is_same<T, double>::value ? x : x.toDouble()); } void f() { std::cout << "a = " << a << '\n'; } }; int main() { mpfr::mpreal x {1.0}; double y {2.0}; { Bla<mpfr::mpreal> a {Bla<mpfr::mpreal>(x)}; a.f(); } { Bla<double> a {Bla<double>(y)}; a.f(); } return 0; } A: The function needs to be correct regardless of whic
